Penguasa merasa segan kepadanya karena kesungguhannya dalam agama dan usahanya; terkadang ia menghadapi penguasa dengan perkataan keras, menasihatinya, memerintahkan kebaikan dan melarang kemungkaran. Penguasa menanggungnya karena kesalehannya. Ini tetap menjadi kondisinya hingga suatu hari Abu al-Hasan Musa (alaihis salam) datang menemuinya ketika ia berada di masjid.
translations.ru: У меня был двоюродный брат по имени Аль-Хасан ибн Абдуллах; он был аскетом и являлся одним из наиболее усердных в поклонении людей своего времени. Правитель побаивался его из-за его серьёзности в религии и его усердия; иногда он встречал правителя с резкими словами, увещевая его, повелевая добро и запрещая зло. Правитель терпел его ради его праведности. Так продолжалось, пока однажды Абу аль-Хасан Муса (мир ему) не пришёл к нему, когда тот был в мечети.
translations.tr: El-Hasan b. Abdullah adında bir amcaoğlum vardı; zâhiddi ve zamanının en çok ibadet eden kişilerindendi. Sultan, onun dindeki ciddiyeti ve çabası nedeniyle ondan çekinirdi; bazen sultana sert sözlerle yüz yüze gelir, onu nasihat eder, iyiliği emreder, kötülükten menedenirdi. Sultan onu sâlihliği nedeniyle hoş görürdü. Ebu'l-Hasan Musa (aleyhisselam) mescidde iken bir gün ona gelene kadar bu hali devam etti.
translations.ur: میرا ایک چچا زاد بھائی تھا جسے حسن بن عبداللہ کہا جاتا تھا؛ وہ زاہد تھا اور اپنے زمانے کے سب سے زیادہ عبادت گزار لوگوں میں سے تھا۔ سلطان اس کے دین میں جدیت اور کوشش کی وجہ سے اس سے ڈرتا تھا؛ کبھی کبھار وہ سلطان کا سامنا سخت الفاظ سے کرتا، اسے نصیحت کرتا، اسے معروف کا حکم دیتا اور منکر سے روکتا۔ سلطان اس کی نیکی کی وجہ سے اسے برداشت کرتا تھا۔ یہی اس کی حالت رہی یہاں تک کہ ایک دن جب وہ مسجد میں تھا تو ابو الحسن موسیٰ علیہ السلام اس کے پاس آئے۔
translations.zh: 我有一位堂兄弟叫哈桑·本·阿卜杜拉，他是个苦行者，是他那个时代最虔诚的礼拜者之一。统治者由于他在宗教方面的认真和努力而对他心存敬畏；有时他会用严厉的话面对统治者，劝诫他，命令他行善，禁止他作恶。统治者因他的正直而容忍他。这一直是他的状态，直到有一天，当他在清真寺时，阿布·哈桑·穆萨（愿主赐他平安）来到他那里。

translations.bn: ইমাম তাকে দেখলেন এবং ইশারা করলেন; তিনি এলেন। তিনি তাকে বললেন: 'হে আবু আলী! তুমি যে কাজে আছ তা আমার কাছে কতই না প্রিয় এবং কতই না আনন্দিত করে — শুধু তোমার মা'রিফা নেই। তাই মা'রিফা অন্বেষণ কর।' তিনি বললেন: 'আমি আপনার জন্য কুরবান! মা'রিফা কী?' তিনি বললেন: 'যাও, ফিকাহ শেখো এবং হাদীস অন্বেষণ কর।' তিনি বললেন: 'কার কাছ থেকে?' তিনি বললেন: 'মদীনার ফকীহদের কাছ থেকে; তারপর হাদীস আমার কাছে উপস্থাপন কর।'
translations.de: Er sah ihn und winkte ihm zu; er kam zu ihm. Er sagte zu ihm: 'O Abu Ali! Wie sehr liegt mir das am Herzen, was du tust, und wie sehr freut es mich — außer dass du keine Ma'rifa hast. Suche also die Ma'rifa.' Er sagte: 'Ich sei dein Lösegeld! Was ist Ma'rifa?' Er sagte: 'Geh, erlerne die Rechtswissenschaft und suche Hadithe.' Er sagte: 'Von wem?' Er sagte: 'Von den Gelehrten der Leute von Medina; dann lege mir die Hadithe vor.'
translations.en: He (the Imam) saw him and gestured to him; he came to him. He said to him: 'O Abu Ali! How beloved to me is what you are engaged in, and how it pleases me — except that you have no ma'rifa. So seek the ma'rifa.' He said: 'May I be your ransom! What is ma'rifa?' He said: 'Go and acquire jurisprudential knowledge and seek hadith.' He said: 'From whom?' He said: 'From the jurists of the people of Medina; then present the hadith to me.'
translations.es: Lo vio y le hizo señas; él fue hacia él. Le dijo: '¡Oh Abu Ali! Cuánto me agrada lo que haces y cuánto me complace — excepto que no tienes ma'rifa. Busca pues la ma'rifa.' Dijo: '¡Sea yo tu rescate! ¿Qué es la ma'rifa?' Dijo: 'Ve, estudia jurisprudencia y busca hadices.' Dijo: '¿De quién?' Dijo: 'De los juristas de la gente de Medina; luego preséntame el hadiz.'
translations.fa: حضرت او را دید و اشاره کرد، او آمد. به او فرمود: «ای ابوعلی! آنچه در آن هستی چقدر برایم محبوب و مسرّت‌بخش است — مگر اینکه تو معرفت نداری. پس معرفت را طلب کن.» گفت: «فدایت شوم! معرفت چیست؟» فرمود: «برو و فقه بیاموز و حدیث طلب کن.» گفت: «از چه کسی؟» فرمود: «از فقهای اهل مدینه، سپس حدیث را نزد من عرضه کن.»
translations.fr: Il le vit et lui fit signe ; il vint à lui. Il lui dit : « Ô Abu Ali ! Combien m'est chère ce que tu fais et combien cela me réjouit — sauf que tu n'as pas de ma'rifa. Cherche donc la ma'rifa. » Il dit : « Que je sois ton sacrifice ! Qu'est-ce que la ma'rifa ? » Il dit : « Va, étudie le fiqh et cherche des hadiths. » Il dit : « Auprès de qui ? » Il dit : « Auprès des juristes des gens de Médine ; puis présente-moi les hadiths. »
translations.id: Beliau melihatnya dan memberinya isyarat; ia pun mendatangi beliau. Beliau berkata kepadanya: 'Wahai Abu Ali! Betapa aku mencintai kondisimu dan betapa senangnya aku — hanya saja engkau tidak memiliki ma'rifa. Maka carilah ma'rifa.' Ia berkata: 'Aku tebus Anda! Apa itu ma'rifa?' Beliau bersabda: 'Pergilah, pelajari fikih, dan carilah hadis.' Ia berkata: 'Dari siapa?' Beliau bersabda: 'Dari para fuqaha penduduk Madinah; kemudian sajikan hadis itu kepadaku.'
translations.ru: Он увидел его и подозвал; тот подошёл к нему. Он сказал ему: «О Абу Али! Как мне дорого то, чем ты занят, и как радует меня это — кроме того, что у тебя нет ма'рифа. Поэтому ищи ма'рифа». Он сказал: «Да буду я твоей жертвой! Что такое ма'рифа?» Он сказал: «Иди, изучай фикх и ищи хадисы». Он сказал: «От кого?» Он сказал: «От факихов жителей Медины; затем представь мне хадисы».
translations.tr: İmam onu görünce işaret etti; o da yanına geldi. Ona dedi ki: 'Ey Ebu Ali! Senin içinde bulunduğun durum bana ne kadar sevimli ve ne kadar sevinç verici — yalnız sende ma'rife yok. Öyleyse ma'rifeti ara.' Dedi: 'Sana feda olayım! Ma'rife nedir?' Buyurdu: 'Git, fıkıh öğren ve hadis talep et.' Dedi: 'Kimden?' Buyurdu: 'Medine halkının fakihlerinden; sonra hadisi bana sun.'
translations.ur: آپ نے اسے دیکھا اور اشارہ کیا، تو وہ آیا۔ آپ نے اس سے فرمایا: 'اے ابو علی! تو جس کام میں ہے وہ مجھے کتنا محبوب ہے اور مجھے کتنی خوشی دیتا ہے — مگر تیرے پاس معرفت نہیں۔ پس معرفت طلب کر۔' اس نے کہا: 'میں آپ پر فدا ہوں! معرفت کیا ہے؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'جاؤ فقہ سیکھو اور حدیث طلب کرو۔' اس نے کہا: 'کس سے؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'اہل مدینہ کے فقہا سے، پھر مجھے حدیث پیش کرو۔'
translations.zh: 伊玛目看见了他并向他示意；他走了过来。伊玛目对他说：'啊，阿布·阿里！你所做的事情多么令我喜爱，多么令我高兴——只是你没有马尔里法。因此要寻求马尔里法。'他说：'愿我为你牺牲！什么是马尔里法？'伊玛目说：'去，学习教法，寻求圣训。'他说：'从谁那里？'伊玛目说：'从麦地那学者那里；然后将圣训呈给我。'

translations.bn: তিনি বললেন: তিনি গেলেন এবং লিখলেন, তারপর এলেন এবং পড়ে শোনালেন। ইমাম সব প্রত্যাখ্যান করলেন, তারপর বললেন: 'যাও এবং সত্যিকারের মা'রিফা অর্জন কর।' সেই ব্যক্তি তার দীনের ব্যাপারে অত্যন্ত উদ্বিগ্ন ছিলেন; তিনি ক্রমাগত আবুল হাসান আলাইহিস সালামের অপেক্ষায় থাকলেন যতক্ষণ না তিনি তার জমিতে বের হলেন; তিনি পথে তার সাথে দেখা করলেন। তিনি বললেন: 'আমি আপনার জন্য কুরবান! আমি আল্লাহর সামনে আপনার বিরুদ্ধে দলিল উপস্থাপন করব — আমাকে মা'রিফার পথ দেখান।'
translations.de: Er sagte: Er ging hin und schrieb sie auf, kam dann und las sie vor. Er (der Imam) verwarf alles, dann sagte er: 'Geh und erlange die wahre Ma'rifa.' Der Mann war ernsthaft um seine Religion bemüht; er hörte nicht auf, Abu al-Hasan (Friede sei mit ihm) aufzulauern, bis er zu seinem Gut hinausging; er begegnete ihm auf dem Weg. Er sagte zu ihm: 'Ich sei dein Lösegeld! Ich werde vor Gott Argumente gegen dich vorbringen — führe mich zur Ma'rifa.'
translations.en: He said: So he went and wrote (them) down, then came to him and recited them. He (the Imam) rejected all of it, then said to him: 'Go and attain the true ma'rifa.' The man was deeply concerned about his religion; he did not cease to watch for Abu al-Hasan (peace be upon him) until he (the Imam) went out to his estate; he met him on the road. He said to him: 'May I be your ransom! I will argue before God with you — guide me to ma'rifa.'
translations.es: Dijo: Fue y los escribió, luego vino y se los recitó. Él (el Imam) los rechazó todos, luego dijo: 'Ve y alcanza la verdadera ma'rifa.' El hombre estaba profundamente preocupado por su religión; no cesó de acechar a Abu al-Hasan (la paz sea con él) hasta que salió a su propiedad; le encontró en el camino. Le dijo: '¡Sea yo tu rescate! Voy a argumentar ante Dios contigo — guíame hacia la ma'rifa.'
translations.fa: گوید: پس رفت و نوشت، سپس آمد و برایش خواند. حضرت همه را رد کرد، سپس فرمود: «برو و معرفت واقعی را بشناس.» آن مرد نسبت به دینش دغدغه‌مند بود، پس پیوسته مراقب ابوالحسن علیه السلام بود تا اینکه به ملکش رفت؛ در راه با او روبرو شد. گفت: «فدایت شوم! در پیشگاه الله با تو احتجاج خواهم کرد — مرا به معرفت راهنمایی کن.»
translations.fr: Il dit : Il alla écrire, puis revint et les récita. Il (l'Imam) les rejeta tous, puis dit : 'Va et obtiens la vraie ma'rifa.' L'homme était profondément soucieux de sa religion ; il ne cessa d'épier Abu al-Hasan (que la paix soit sur lui) jusqu'à ce qu'il sorte vers sa propriété ; il le rencontra sur la route. Il lui dit : 'Que je sois ton sacrifice ! Je vais argumenter devant Dieu contre toi — guide-moi vers la ma'rifa.'
translations.id: Ia berkata: maka ia pergi dan menulis, lalu datang dan membacakannya. Beliau menolak semuanya, lalu bersabda: 'Pergi dan kenalilah ma'rifa yang sejati.' Orang itu sangat peduli dengan agamanya; ia terus mengintai Abu al-Hasan (alaihis salam) sampai beliau keluar menuju tanahnya; ia bertemu dengannya di jalan. Ia berkata: 'Aku tebus Anda! Aku akan berargumen di hadapan Allah — tunjukkanlah aku kepada ma'rifa.'
translations.ru: Он сказал: он пошёл и записал, затем пришёл и прочитал ему. Он (Имам) отверг всё, затем сказал: 'Иди и обрети истинное ма'рифа'. Этот человек был глубоко озабочен своей религией; он не переставал следить за Абу аль-Хасаном (мир ему), пока тот не вышел в своё имение; он встретил его на дороге. Он сказал ему: 'Да буду я твоей жертвой! Я буду спорить пред Аллахом с тобой — укажи мне на ма'рифа'.
translations.tr: Dedi ki: Gidip yazdı, sonra yanına geldi ve okudu. İmam tamamını reddetti, ardından dedi: 'Git ve gerçek ma'rifeti bul.' Adam dinine çok düşkündü; Ebu'l-Hasan'ı (a.s.) gözetlemeyi bırakmadı; nihayet o kendi arazisine çıkınca yolda karşıladı. Ona dedi: 'Sana feda olayım! Allah huzurunda seninle hüccetleşeceğim — beni ma'rifeye yönlendir.'
translations.ur: اس نے کہا: تو وہ گیا اور لکھا، پھر اس کے پاس آیا اور پڑھ کر سنایا۔ آپ نے سب رد کر دیا، پھر فرمایا: 'جاؤ اور حقیقی معرفت حاصل کرو۔' وہ شخص اپنے دین کے بارے میں فکرمند تھا، پس برابر ابو الحسن علیہ السلام کی تاک میں رہا یہاں تک کہ آپ اپنی جاگیر کی طرف نکلے، اس نے راستے میں ملاقات کی اور کہا: 'میں آپ پر فدا ہوں! میں اللہ کے سامنے آپ سے حجت کروں گا — مجھے معرفت کی طرف راہنمائی کریں۔'
translations.zh: 他说：于是他去了，写下了圣训，然后回来读给他听。伊玛目全部驳回，然后说：'去，获得真正的马尔里法。'这个人非常关心他的宗教；他不断守候阿布·哈桑（愿主赐他平安），直到他前往自己的庄园；在路上遇见了他。他说：'愿我为你牺牲！我将在真主面前与你辩论——请引导我找到马尔里法。'

translations.bn: তিনি বললেন: তিনি (ইমাম) তাকে আমীরুল মু'মিনীন আলাইহিস সালাম সম্পর্কে এবং রাসূলুল্লাহ সাল্লাল্লাহু আলাইহি ওয়াআলিহির পরে যা ঘটেছিল তা জানালেন, এবং দুই ব্যক্তির বিষয় জানালেন। তিনি তা গ্রহণ করলেন। তারপর বললেন: 'আমীরুল মু'মিনীন আলাইহিস সালামের পর কে ছিলেন?' ইমাম বললেন: 'হাসান আলাইহিস সালাম, তারপর হুসাইন আলাইহিস সালাম' — নিজ পর্যন্ত, তারপর চুপ করলেন। তিনি বললেন: 'আমি কুরবান! আজকের ইমাম কে?' ইমাম বললেন: 'যদি বলি, মানবে?' বললেন: 'হ্যাঁ, কুরবান হই!' ইমাম বললেন: 'আমি।'
translations.de: Er sagte: Er (der Imam) informierte ihn über den Befehlshaber der Gläubigen (Friede sei mit ihm) und was nach dem Gesandten Gottes (Friede sei mit ihm und seiner Familie) geschah, und informierte ihn über die Angelegenheit der zwei Männer (Abu Bakr und Umar). Er akzeptierte das von ihm. Dann sagte er: 'Wer kam nach dem Befehlshaber der Gläubigen (Friede sei mit ihm)?' Er sagte: 'Al-Hasan (Friede sei mit ihm), dann Al-Husayn (Friede sei mit ihm)', bis er zu sich selbst gelangte; dann schwieg er. Er sagte: 'Ich sei dein Lösegeld! Wer ist der Imam heute?' Er sagte: 'Wenn ich es dir sage, wirst du es akzeptieren?' Er sagte: 'Ja, ich sei dein Lösegeld!' Er sagte: 'Ich bin er.'
translations.en: He said: So he (the Imam) informed him about the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) and what happened after the Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family), and informed him about the matter of the two men (Abu Bakr and Umar). He accepted this from him. Then he said to him: 'Who was after the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)?' He said: 'Al-Hasan (peace be upon him), then Al-Husayn (peace be upon him),' until he reached himself; then he fell silent. He said: He then said to him: 'May I be your ransom! Who is the Imam today?' He said: 'If I inform you, will you accept?' He said: 'Yes, may I be your ransom!' He said: 'I am he.'
translations.es: Dijo: Él (el Imam) le informó sobre el Comandante de los Creyentes (la paz sea con él) y lo que ocurrió tras el Mensajero de Dios (la paz sea con él y su familia), e informó sobre el asunto de los dos hombres (Abu Bakr y Umar). Aceptó esto de él. Luego dijo: '¿Quién vino después del Comandante de los Creyentes (la paz sea con él)?' Dijo: 'Al-Hasan (la paz sea con él), luego Al-Husayn (la paz sea con él),' hasta llegar a sí mismo; luego se calló. Dijo: '¡Sea yo tu rescate! ¿Quién es el Imam hoy?' Dijo: 'Si te lo digo, ¿lo aceptarás?' Dijo: '¡Sí, sea yo tu rescate!' Dijo: 'Yo soy él.'
translations.fa: گوید: پس از امیرالمؤمنین علیه السلام و آنچه پس از رسول الله صلی الله علیه وآله اتفاق افتاده بود به او خبر داد و از امر دو نفر به او گفت. پذیرفت. سپس گفت: «بعد از امیرالمؤمنین علیه السلام چه کسی بود؟» فرمود: «حسن علیه السلام، سپس حسین علیه السلام» — تا به خودش رسید، سپس ساکت شد. گفت: «فدایت شوم! امروز امام کیست؟» فرمود: «اگر بگویم می‌پذیری؟» گفت: «بله، فدایت!» فرمود: «من هستم.»
translations.fr: Il dit : L'Imam l'informa au sujet du Commandeur des Croyants (que la paix soit sur lui) et de ce qui se passa après le Messager de Dieu (que la paix soit sur lui et sa famille), et l'informa de l'affaire des deux hommes (Abu Bakr et Umar). Il accepta. Puis il dit : 'Qui vint après le Commandeur des Croyants (que la paix soit sur lui) ?' Il dit : 'Al-Hasan (que la paix soit sur lui), puis Al-Husayn (que la paix soit sur lui)', jusqu'à en arriver à lui-même ; puis il se tut. Il dit : 'Que je sois ton sacrifice ! Qui est l'Imam aujourd'hui ?' Il dit : 'Si je te le dis, l'accepteras-tu ?' Il dit : 'Oui, que je sois ton sacrifice !' Il dit : 'C'est moi.'
translations.id: Ia berkata: maka beliau memberitahunya tentang Amirul Mukminin (a.s.) dan apa yang terjadi setelah Rasulullah (s.a.a.), serta tentang perkara dua orang itu (Abu Bakr dan Umar). Ia menerimanya. Lalu ia berkata: 'Siapa yang datang setelah Amirul Mukminin (a.s.)?' Beliau bersabda: 'Al-Hasan (a.s.), kemudian Al-Husain (a.s.),' sampai beliau tiba pada dirinya sendiri; lalu beliau diam. Ia berkata: 'Aku tebus Anda! Siapa imam hari ini?' Beliau bersabda: 'Jika aku memberitahumu, apakah kamu menerimanya?' Ia berkata: 'Ya, aku tebus Anda!' Beliau bersabda: 'Aku-lah.'
translations.ru: Он сказал: Он (Имам) сообщил ему об Амире верующих (мир ему) и о том, что произошло после Посланника Аллаха (мир ему и его семье), и рассказал о деле двух мужей (Абу Бакра и Умара). Он принял это от него. Затем спросил: 'Кто был после Амира верующих (мир ему)?' Он ответил: 'Аль-Хасан (мир ему), затем Аль-Хусайн (мир ему)' — пока не дошёл до самого себя; затем замолчал. Тот сказал: 'Да буду я твоей жертвой! Кто Имам сегодня?' Он сказал: 'Если я скажу тебе, примешь ли ты?' Он сказал: 'Да, да буду я твоей жертвой!' Он сказал: 'Я — он'.
translations.tr: Dedi ki: İmam ona Emiru'l-Müminin (a.s.) hakkında ve Allah Resulü'nün (s.a.a.) vefatından sonra olanları anlattı; iki adamın (Ebu Bekir ve Ömer) meselesini de anlattı. Kabul etti. Sonra: 'Emiru'l-Müminin'den (a.s.) sonra kim geldi?' dedi. İmam: 'Hasan (a.s.), sonra Hüseyin (a.s.)' dedi; kendisine gelinceye kadar, sonra sustu. Adam: 'Sana feda olayım! Bugün imam kimdir?' dedi. İmam: 'Söylesem kabul eder misin?' dedi. Adam: 'Evet, feda olayım!' dedi. İmam: 'Benim' buyurdu.
translations.ur: راوی نے کہا: پس آپ نے اسے امیر المومنین علیہ السلام کے بارے میں اور رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ کے بعد جو کچھ ہوا اس کے بارے میں بتایا، اور دونوں آدمیوں کے معاملے کے بارے میں بتایا۔ اس نے قبول کیا۔ پھر اس نے کہا: 'امیر المومنین علیہ السلام کے بعد کون تھے؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'حسن علیہ السلام، پھر حسین علیہ السلام' — خود تک پہنچ کر چپ ہو گئے۔ اس نے کہا: 'میں آپ پر فدا ہوں! آج امام کون ہیں؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'اگر بتاؤں تو مانو گے؟' اس نے کہا: 'ہاں، فدا ہوں!' آپ نے فرمایا: 'میں وہی ہوں۔'
translations.zh: 他说：伊玛目告诉了他关于信士的长官（愿主赐他平安）以及使者（愿主赐福于他和他的家属）归真后所发生的事情，并告诉他两人（阿布·伯克尔和欧麦尔）的情况。他接受了。然后他说：'信士的长官（愿主赐他平安）之后是谁？'他说：'哈桑（愿主赐他平安），然后是侯赛因（愿主赐他平安）'，直到说到了他自己；然后沉默了。他说：'愿我为你牺牲！今日谁是伊玛目？'伊玛目说：'如果我告诉你，你会接受吗？'他说：'是的，愿我为你牺牲！'伊玛目说：'我就是。'

translations.bn: তিনি বললেন: 'কোনো প্রমাণ আছে?' তিনি (ইমাম) বললেন: 'ঐ গাছের কাছে যাও' — এবং হাতে উম্ম গাইলান (এক ধরনের বাবলা গাছ) ইশারা করলেন — 'এবং তাকে বলো: মুসা ইবনে জা'ফার তোমাকে আসতে বলেছেন।' বললেন: আমি গেলাম এবং আল্লাহর কসম দেখলাম সে মাটি ভেদ করে এগিয়ে আসছে যতক্ষণ না ইমামের সামনে দাঁড়িয়ে পড়ল। তারপর ইমাম ইশারা করলেন এবং সে ফিরে গেল। তিনি বললেন: তিনি ইমামকে স্বীকার করলেন এবং নীরবতা ও ইবাদতকে নিজের উপর আবশ্যক করে নিলেন; এরপর কেউ তাকে কথা বলতে দেখেনি।
translations.de: Er sagte: 'Gibt es etwas, womit ich Beweis führen kann?' Er (der Imam) sagte: 'Geh zu jenem Baum,' — und er zeigte mit seiner Hand auf den Umm Ghaylan (eine Art Akazienbaum) — 'und sage ihm: Musa ibn Ja'far befiehlt dir zu kommen.' Er sagte: Ich ging hin und bei Gott sah ich, wie er die Erde aufpflügte, bis er vor dem Imam stand. Dann winkte ihm der Imam und er kehrte zurück. Er sagte: Er erkannte ihn an und widmete sich dem Schweigen und der Andacht; niemand sah ihn danach mehr sprechen.
translations.en: He said: 'Is there something I can use as evidence?' He (the Imam) said: 'Go to that tree,' — and he gestured with his hand toward the Umm Ghaylan (a type of acacia tree) — 'and say to it: Musa ibn Ja'far commands you to come forward.' He said: So I went to it and, I swear by God, I saw it plowing through the earth until it stopped before him. Then he gestured to it and it returned. He said: He acknowledged him and thereafter devoted himself to silence and worship; no one saw him speak after that.
translations.es: Dijo: '¿Hay algo con lo que pueda obtener evidencia?' Él (el Imam) dijo: 'Ve a ese árbol,' — y señaló con su mano hacia el Umm Ghaylan (tipo de acacia) — 'y dile: Musa ibn Ja'far te ordena que vengas.' Dijo: Fui y, juro por Dios, lo vi surcar la tierra hasta detenerse frente a él. Luego el Imam le hizo señas y regresó. Dijo: Lo reconoció y se dedicó al silencio y a la adoración; nadie le vio hablar después de eso.
translations.fa: گفت: «آیا چیزی هست که بتوانم با آن استدلال کنم؟» فرمود: «به آن درخت برو» — و با دستش به ام غیلان اشاره کرد — «و به آن بگو: موسی بن جعفر می‌گوید بیا.» گوید: پس نزدش رفتم و به خدا دیدم که زمین را می‌شکافت تا در برابر حضرت ایستاد. سپس حضرت اشاره کرد و برگشت. گوید: اقرار کرد به او، سپس سکوت و عبادت را بر خود لازم گرداند و پس از آن کسی او را در حال کلام کردن ندید.
translations.fr: Il dit : 'Y a-t-il quelque chose dont je puisse me servir comme preuve ?' Il (l'Imam) dit : 'Va vers cet arbre,' — et il montra de la main le Umm Ghaylan (un type d'acacia) — 'et dis-lui : Musa ibn Ja'far te commande de venir.' Il dit : J'y allai et, je jure par Dieu, je le vis labourer la terre jusqu'à s'arrêter devant lui. Puis l'Imam lui fit signe et il revint. Il dit : Il le reconnut et s'astreignit au silence et à la dévotion ; personne ne le vit plus parler après cela.
translations.id: Ia berkata: 'Adakah sesuatu yang bisa kujadikan bukti?' Beliau bersabda: 'Pergilah ke pohon itu,' — dan beliau menunjuk dengan tangannya ke pohon Umm Ghaylan — 'dan katakan kepadanya: Musa bin Ja'far memerintahkanmu untuk datang.' Ia berkata: Maka aku mendatanginya dan demi Allah aku melihatnya mengarungi tanah sampai berhenti di hadapan beliau. Kemudian beliau memberi isyarat dan pohon itu kembali. Ia berkata: Ia mengakui beliau, lalu menjadikan diam dan ibadah sebagai kewajibannya; tidak ada yang melihatnya berbicara setelah itu.
translations.ru: Он сказал: 'Есть ли что-нибудь, чем можно доказать?' Он (Имам) сказал: 'Иди к тому дереву,' — и указал рукой на Умм Гайлан (вид акации) — 'и скажи ему: Муса ибн Джа'фар велит тебе подойти.' Он сказал: Я пошёл и, клянусь Аллахом, видел, как оно вспарывало землю, пока не остановилось перед ним. Затем Имам сделал ему знак, и оно вернулось. Он сказал: Он признал его и посвятил себя молчанию и поклонению; никто не видел его говорящим после того.
translations.tr: Dedi: 'Delil olarak kullanabileceğim bir şey var mı?' İmam buyurdu: 'Şu ağaca git' — ve eliyle Ümmü Gaylân'a işaret etti — 've ona söyle: Musa b. Ca'fer sana gelmeni emrediyor.' Ravî dedi: Gittim ve Allah'a yemin olsun toprağı yararak ilerleyip İmam'ın önünde durduğunu gördüm. Sonra İmam işaret etti ve geri döndü. Ravî dedi: İmamı kabul etti, ardından sükûtu ve ibadeti üzerine aldı; kimse onu bundan sonra konuşurken görmedi.
translations.ur: اس نے کہا: 'کوئی چیز ہے جس سے دلیل لے سکوں؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'اس درخت کے پاس جاؤ' — اور ہاتھ سے ام غیلان کی طرف اشارہ کیا — 'اور اس سے کہو: موسیٰ بن جعفر کہتے ہیں تم آگے آؤ۔' راوی نے کہا: تو میں اس کے پاس گیا اور واللہ میں نے دیکھا کہ وہ زمین کاٹتی ہوئی آئی یہاں تک کہ آپ کے سامنے کھڑی ہو گئی۔ پھر آپ نے اشارہ کیا تو واپس چلی گئی۔ راوی نے کہا: اس نے آپ کو مان لیا، پھر خاموشی اور عبادت کو لازم کر لیا، اور اس کے بعد کوئی اسے بات کرتے نہیں دیکھا۔
translations.zh: 他说：'有什么证据吗？'他（伊玛目）说：'去那棵树'——并用手指向乌姆·盖兰树（一种金合欢树）——'对它说：穆萨·本·贾法尔命令你过来。'他说：我去了，奉真主发誓，我看见它犁开地面，直到停在他面前。然后伊玛目向它示意，它就回去了。他说：他承认了伊玛目，此后便沉默并专心礼拜，从此没有人看见他再开口说话。

translations.bn: মুহাম্মাদ ইবনে ইয়াহইয়া এবং আহমাদ ইবনে মুহাম্মাদ, মুহাম্মাদ ইবনুল হাসান থেকে, তিনি ইব্রাহীম ইবনে হাশিম থেকে — একইভাবে।
translations.de: Muhammad ibn Yahya und Ahmad ibn Muhammad, von Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, von Ibrahim ibn Hashim — ähnlich.
translations.en: Muhammad ibn Yahya and Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, from Ibrahim ibn Hashim — similar to it.
translations.es: Muhammad ibn Yahya y Ahmad ibn Muhammad, de Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, de Ibrahim ibn Hashim — similar a esto.
translations.fa: محمد بن یحیی و احمد بن محمد از محمد بن الحسن، از ابراهیم بن هاشم — به همین مضمون روایت کرده‌اند.
translations.fr: Muhammad ibn Yahya et Ahmad ibn Muhammad, de Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, d'Ibrahim ibn Hashim — similaire à cela.
translations.id: Muhammad bin Yahya dan Ahmad bin Muhammad, dari Muhammad bin al-Hasan, dari Ibrahim bin Hashim — serupa dengan itu.
translations.ru: Мухаммад ибн Яхья и Ахмад ибн Мухаммад — от Мухаммада ибн аль-Хасана — от Ибрахима ибн Хашима — аналогичным образом.
translations.tr: Muhammed b. Yahya ve Ahmed b. Muhammed, Muhammed b. el-Hasan'dan, o İbrahim b. Haşim'den — benzer şekilde rivayet etmiştir.
translations.ur: محمد بن یحییٰ اور احمد بن محمد نے محمد بن الحسن سے، انہوں نے ابراہیم بن ہاشم سے — اسی طرح روایت کی ہے۔
translations.zh: 穆罕默德·本·叶海亚和艾哈迈德·本·穆罕默德，传自穆罕默德·本·哈桑，传自易卜拉欣·本·哈希姆——与此相似。

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ فُلَانٍ الْوَاقِفِيِّ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: كَانَ لِيَ ابْنُ عَمٍّ يُقَالُ لَهُ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ كَانَ زَاهِداً وَكَانَ مِنْ أَعْبَدِ أَهْلِ زَمَانِهِ وَكَانَ يَتَّقِيهِ السُّلْطَانُ لِجِدِّهِ فِي الدِّينِ وَاجْتِهَادِهِ وَرُبَّمَا اسْتَقْبَلَ السُّلْطَانَ بِكَلَامٍ صَعْبٍ يَعِظُهُ وَيَأْمُرُهُ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَاهُ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَكَانَ السُّلْطَانُ يَحْتَمِلُهُ لِصَلَاحِهِ وَلَمْ تَزَلْ هَذِهِ حَالَتَهُ حَتَّى كَانَ يَوْمٌ مِنَ الْأَيَّامِ إِذْ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ مُوسَى عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ وَهُوَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فَرَآهُ فَأَوْمَأَ إِلَيْهِ فَأَتَاهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ يَا أَبَا عَلِيٍّ مَا أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ مَا أَنْتَ فِيهِ وَأَسَرَّنِي إِلَّا أَنَّهُ لَيْسَتْ لَكَ مَعْرِفَةٌ فَاطْلُبِ الْمَعْرِفَةَ قَالَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ وَمَا الْمَعْرِفَةُ قَالَ اذْهَبْ فَتَفَقَّهْ وَاطْلُبِ الْحَدِيثَ قَالَ عَمَّنْ قَالَ عَنْ فُقَهَاءِ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ ثُمَّ اعْرِضْ عَلَيَّ الْحَدِيثَ قَالَ فَذَهَبَ فَكَتَبَ ثُمَّ جَاءَهُ فَقَرَأَهُ عَلَيْهِ فَأَسْقَطَهُ كُلَّهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ اذْهَبْ فَاعْرِفِ الْمَعْرِفَةَ وَكَانَ الرَّجُلُ مَعْنِيّاً بِدِينِهِ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ يَتَرَصَّدُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ حَتَّى خَرَجَ إِلَى ضَيْعَةٍ لَهُ فَلَقِيَهُ فِي الطَّرِيقِ فَقَالَ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ إِنِّي أَحْتَجُّ عَلَيْكَ بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ فَدُلَّنِي عَلَى الْمَعْرِفَةِ قَالَ فَأَخْبَرَهُ بِأَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ وَمَا كَانَ بَعْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ وَمَا أَخْبَرَهُ بِأَمْرِ الرَّجُلَيْنِ فَقَبِلَ مِنْهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ فَمَنْ كَانَ بَعْدَ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ الْحَسَنُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ ثُمَّ الْحُسَيْنُ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ حَتَّى انْتَهَى إِلَى نَفْسِهِ ثُمَّ سَكَتَ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ فَمَنْ هُوَ الْيَوْمَ قَالَ إِنْ أَخْبَرْتُكَ تَقْبَلُ قَالَ بَلَى جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ أَنَا هُوَ قَالَ فَشَيْءٌ أَسْتَدِلُّ بِهِ قَالَ اذْهَبْ إِلَى تِلْكَ الشَّجَرَةِ وَأَشَارَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى أُمِّ غَيْلَانَ فَقُلْ لَهَا يَقُولُ لَكِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ أَقْبِلِي قَالَ فَأَتَيْتُهَا فَرَأَيْتُهَا وَاللَّهِ تَخُدُّ الْأَرْضَ خَدّاً حَتَّى وَقَفَتْ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ثُمَّ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهَا فَرَجَعَتْ قَالَ فَأَقَرَّ بِهِ ثُمَّ لَزِمَ الصَّمْتَ وَالْعِبَادَةَ فَكَانَ لَا يَرَاهُ أَحَدٌ يَتَكَلَّمُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ

narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Ibrahim al-Qummi, prominent Shia scholar and author of Tafsir al-Qummi (d. after 307 AH)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Refers to Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, father of Ali ibn Ibrahim al-Qummi
narrators.known_identity: Ibrahim ibn Hashim al-Qummi, a major hadith transmitter of the third generation

narrators.ambiguity_note: Identified in parallel chain (closing) as Muhammad ibn al-Hasan. Likely Muhammad ibn al-Hasan al-Saffar or another Muhammad in the chain.
narrators.known_identity: Likely Muhammad ibn al-Hasan, as indicated by the parallel chain at the end of the hadith

narrators.ambiguity_note: Name is deliberately obscured (Fulan = 'so-and-so'). Designated as Waqifi — a member of the Waqifiyya sect who believed the Imamate ended with the seventh Imam Musa al-Kazim. Despite his sectarian affiliation, his narration is transmitted here.

key_phrases.phrase_en: May I be your ransom/sacrifice (expression of devotion to the Imam)
Commander of the Faithful (title of Imam Ali)
Jurists of the people of Medina
Abu al-Hasan Musa (kunya of Imam Musa al-Kazim)

bn.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: উম্ম গাইলান — এক ধরনের বাবলা গাছ যা ইমামের আদেশে হেঁটে এল
bn.الْوَاقِفِيّ: ওয়াকিফী — যে বিশ্বাস করে ইমামত ইমাম মুসা কাযিমের সাথে শেষ হয়েছে
bn.مَعْرِفَة: মা'রিফা — সত্যিকারের ইমামের পরিচিতি, ইমামতের জ্ঞান

de.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: Umm Ghaylan — Akazienart, die wunderbarerweise dem Befehl des Imams gehorchte
de.الْوَاقِفِيّ: Waqifi — jemand, der glaubt, das Imamat endete mit Imam Musa al-Kazim
de.مَعْرِفَة: Ma'rifa — Erkenntnis des rechtmäßigen Imams, Gnosis des Imamats

en.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: Umm Ghaylan — a type of acacia tree (Faidherbia albida), used here as a miraculous sign confirming the Imam's authority
en.الْأَمْرُ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّهْيُ عَن: Enjoining good and forbidding evil — the Islamic obligation of moral accountability in society
en.الْوَاقِفِيّ: Waqifi — member of the Waqifiyya sect who believed the Imamate ended with Imam Musa al-Kazim
en.جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ: A phrase of deep reverence meaning 'may I be sacrificed/ransomed for you', used as a form of address to the Imam
en.مَعْرِفَة: Gnosis, recognition — specifically the recognition of the rightful Imam as a spiritual and religious obligation

es.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: Umm Ghaylan — tipo de árbol acacia que milagrosamente obedeció la orden del Imam
es.الْوَاقِفِيّ: Waqifi — quien cree que el Imamate terminó con el Imam Musa al-Kazim
es.مَعْرِفَة: Ma'rifa — reconocimiento del Imam legítimo, gnosis del Imamate

fa.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: ام غیلان — نوعی درخت اقاقیا که معجزه‌وار به فرمان امام حرکت کرد
fa.الْوَاقِفِيّ: واقفی — کسی که امامت را با موسی کاظم پایان‌یافته می‌داند
fa.مَعْرِفَة: معرفت — شناخت امام بر حق، گنوسیس امامت

fr.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: Umm Ghaylan — type d'acacia qui obéit miraculeusement à l'ordre de l'Imam
fr.الْوَاقِفِيّ: Waqifi — celui qui croit que l'Imamat s'est terminé avec l'Imam Musa al-Kazim
fr.مَعْرِفَة: Ma'rifa — reconnaissance de l'Imam légitime, gnose de l'Imamat

id.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: Umm Ghaylan — jenis pohon akasia yang secara mukjizat berjalan ke hadapan Imam
id.الْوَاقِفِيّ: Waqifi — orang yang meyakini Imamat berakhir pada Imam Musa al-Kazim
id.مَعْرِفَة: Ma'rifa — pengenalan imam yang sah, gnosis Imamat

ru.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: Умм Гайлан — вид акации, чудесным образом послушавшейся приказа Имама
ru.الْوَاقِفِيّ: Вакифи — тот, кто считает, что Имамат завершился с Имамом Мусой аль-Казимом
ru.مَعْرِفَة: Ма'рифа — познание законного Имама, гносис Имамата

tr.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: Ümmü Gaylân — bir tür akasya ağacı, imamlığı kanıtlayan mucizevî işaret
tr.الْوَاقِفِيّ: Vakıfî — İmameti Musa el-Kazım ile sona erdiğini kabul edenlerden
tr.مَعْرِفَة: Ma'rife — gerçek imamın tanınması, İmamet gnozisi

ur.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: ام غیلان — ایک قسم کا کیکر کا درخت جو معجزانہ طور پر چل کر آیا
ur.الْوَاقِفِيّ: واقفی — وہ شخص جو امام موسیٰ کاظم پر امامت کا سلسلہ ختم سمجھتا ہو
ur.مَعْرِفَة: معرفت — امام کی پہچان اور ولایت کا علم جو نجات کے لیے ضروری ہے

zh.أُمُّ غَيْلَانَ: 乌姆·盖兰——一种金合欢树，奇迹般地服从了伊玛目的命令
zh.الْوَاقِفِيّ: 瓦基菲派——认为伊玛目制度在穆萨·卡兹姆时结束的人
zh.مَعْرِفَة: 马尔里法——对合法伊玛目的认知，伊玛目制度的灵知

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম মুসা কাযিম তার ইমামত প্রমাণে কোন মু'জিযা দেখিয়েছিলেন?
seo_questions.de: Welches Wunder vollbrachte Imam Musa al-Kazim, um sein Imamat zu beweisen?
seo_questions.en: What miracle did Imam Musa al-Kazim perform to prove his Imamate to a devout ascetic?
seo_questions.es: ¿Qué milagro realizó el Imam Musa al-Kazim para probar su Imamate?
seo_questions.fa: امام موسی کاظم برای اثبات امامت خود چه معجزه‌ای نشان داد؟
seo_questions.fr: Quel miracle l'Imam Musa al-Kazim accomplit-il pour prouver son Imamat ?
seo_questions.id: Mukjizat apa yang dilakukan Imam Musa al-Kazim untuk membuktikan keimamatan beliau?
seo_questions.ru: Какое чудо совершил Имам Муса аль-Казим для доказательства своего Имамата?
seo_questions.tr: İmam Musa el-Kazım imamlığını hangi mucize ile ispat etmiştir?
seo_questions.ur: امام موسیٰ کاظم علیہ السلام نے اپنی امامت ثابت کرنے کے لیے کون سا معجزہ دکھایا؟
seo_questions.zh: 伊玛目穆萨·卡兹姆用什么奇迹证明了他的伊玛目地位？

similar_content_hints.description: Hadiths about seekers who came to an Imam asking about the true gnosis (ma'rifa) of the Imamate, and the Imam's response that mere outward worship without recognition of the Imam is incomplete.
Accounts of miracles performed by Imam Musa al-Kazim (AS) — such as speaking to animals, commanding objects, or other supernatural signs — to demonstrate his Imamate to doubters.
Narrations about the Waqifiyya sect and their eventual recognition of or continued rejection of Imam Ali al-Ridha's authority, especially those involving direct encounters with the eighth Imam.

summaries.bn: এই হাদীসটি বর্ণনা করে যে ইমাম মুসা আল-কাযিম আলাইহিস সালাম কীভাবে একজন অত্যন্ত ধার্মিক কিন্তু সত্যিকারের মা'রিফাহীন ব্যক্তিকে ইমামতের সঠিক জ্ঞানের দিকে পরিচালিত করেছিলেন। ইমাম প্রথমে তাকে মদীনার ফকীহদের কাছে পাঠালেন, তারপর সব হাদীস প্রত্যাখ্যান করলেন এবং ইমামদের ধারা উন্মোচন করলেন। উম্ম গাইলান বৃক্ষের মির্যাকল ইমামের পরিচয় নিশ্চিত করল।
summaries.de: Dieser Hadith berichtet, wie Imam Musa al-Kazim (Friede sei mit ihm) einen frommen Asketen ohne ma'rifa zur wahren Erkenntnis des Imamats leitete. Der Imam schickte ihn zunächst zu den Gelehrten Medinas und verwarf alle gesammelten Hadithe, um zu zeigen, dass wahres religiöses Wissen nur durch die Anerkennung der Imame gewonnen werden kann. Das Wunder des Akazienbaums, der auf den Befehl des Imams gehorchte, war der entscheidende Beweis.
summaries.en: This hadith narrates how Imam Musa al-Kazim (peace be upon him) guided a devout but misguided cousin of his narrator, Al-Hasan ibn Abdullah, toward the true gnosis (ma'rifa) of the Imamate. Despite Al-Hasan's exceptional piety and his practice of enjoining good and forbidding evil even toward the ruler, the Imam recognized that outward worship without recognition of the rightful Imam was spiritually incomplete. The Imam first directed him to study hadith from Medinan jurists — deliberately leading him to a dead end — and only when Al-Hasan persisted did the Imam reveal the chain of Imams culminating in himself, and confirmed his Imamate by miraculously commanding an acacia tree to uproot itself and walk to him. Al-Hasan's subsequent embrace of silence and devoted worship indicates the profundity of his conversion. The narrator is identified as a Waqifi — one who believed the Imamate ended with the seventh Imam — making this account particularly striking as self-testimony about the eighth Imam's authority.
summaries.es: Este hadiz narra cómo el Imam Musa al-Kazim (la paz sea con él) guió a un asceta devoto pero carente de ma'rifa hacia el verdadero reconocimiento del Imamate. El Imam primero le dirigió hacia los juristas de Medina y rechazó todos los hadices que recopiló, mostrando que el verdadero conocimiento religioso no puede provenir de fuentes que ignoran a los Imams. El milagro del árbol acacia que respondió a la orden del Imam fue la señal final que llevó al hombre a la plena conversión y al silencio devoto.
summaries.fa: این روایت بیانگر آن است که امام موسی کاظم علیه السلام، پسر عموی راوی را که از زاهدترین مردم زمان خود بود، به سوی معرفت حقیقی یعنی شناخت امام بر حق راهنمایی فرمود. امام ابتدا او را به سوی فقهای مدینه فرستاد و سپس همه آن روایات را رد کرد تا نشان دهد معرفت واقعی تنها از طریق شناخت اهل بیت به دست می‌آید. معجزه درخت ام غیلان که با فرمان امام به سویش حرکت کرد، نشانه‌ای آشکار بر امامت ایشان بود.
summaries.fr: Ce hadith raconte comment l'Imam Musa al-Kazim (que la paix soit sur lui) a conduit un ascète pieux mais dépourvu de ma'rifa vers la véritable reconnaissance de l'Imamat. L'Imam l'a d'abord envoyé chez les juristes de Médine, puis a rejeté tous les hadiths rapportés, montrant que la vraie connaissance religieuse ne peut venir que par la reconnaissance des Imams. Le miracle de l'arbre acacia qui répondit à l'ordre de l'Imam fut la preuve décisive qui conduisit cet homme à une conversion complète.
summaries.id: Hadis ini meriwayatkan bagaimana Imam Musa al-Kazim (alaihis salam) membimbing seorang zahid yang saleh namun tidak mengenal ma'rifa menuju pengakuan Imamat yang benar. Sang Imam lebih dulu mengarahkannya kepada para fuqaha Madinah — kemudian menolak semua riwayat yang dipelajarinya — baru kemudian mengungkapkan rantai para Imam. Mukjizat pohon akasia yang bergerak atas perintah beliau menjadi bukti yang membuatnya beriman sepenuhnya.
summaries.ru: Этот хадис повествует о том, как Имам Муса аль-Казим (мир ему) наставил благочестивого аскета, лишённого ма'рифа, к истинному познанию Имамата. Имам сначала направил его к учёным Медины и отверг все собранные хадисы, показав, что истинное религиозное знание достигается только через признание Имамов. Чудо с акациевым деревом, подчинившимся слову Имама, стало окончательным доказательством его полномочий.
summaries.tr: Bu hadis, Musa b. Fulan el-Vakıfî tarafından rivayet edilmektedir ve İmam Musa el-Kazım'ın (aleyhisselam) derin bir zahid olan El-Hasan b. Abdullah'ı gerçek ma'rifeye — yani İmamet'in tanınmasına — nasıl yönlendirdiğini anlatmaktadır. İmam, önce onu Medine fakihlerinin hadislerini öğrenmeye yönlendirmiş, ardından hepsini reddetmiş; sonunda Ehl-i Beyt'in İmamet silsilesini açıklamış ve bir ağacı mucizevî şekilde emrine boyun eğdirerek İmamet'ini ispat etmiştir. Bu anlatı, Vakıfî bir kaynak tarafından aktarıldığı için özellikle dikkat çekicidir.
summaries.ur: یہ حدیث بیان کرتی ہے کہ امام موسیٰ کاظم علیہ السلام نے راوی کے چچا زاد بھائی حسن بن عبداللہ کو، جو ظاہری عبادت میں بے مثال تھے، امامت کی حقیقی معرفت کی طرف کیسے راہنمائی فرمائی۔ امام نے انہیں پہلے مدینے کے فقہا کی احادیث سیکھنے کا حکم دیا، پھر سب کو رد کر دیا اور انہیں حقیقی معرفت یعنی اہل بیت کی ولایت کی طرف متوجہ کیا۔ ام غیلان کے درخت کا معجزہ جو آپ کی آواز پر چل کر آ گیا، آپ کی امامت کی تصدیق تھی جسے دیکھ کر وہ ایمان لے آئے۔
summaries.zh: 这段圣训记述了伊玛目穆萨·卡兹姆（愿主赐他平安）如何引导一位虔诚的苦行者走向对伊玛目职位（伊玛目）的真正认知（马尔里法）。伊玛目先将他引向麦地那学者，然后驳回了所有收集的圣训，表明真正的宗教知识只能通过承认圣裔家族的伊玛目来获得。金合欢树奉命而来的奇迹是最终的证明，使那人完全信服并归于沉默与礼拜。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول بسندي ه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (97/ 4)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: كَانَ لِيَ ابْنُ عَمٍّ يُقَالُ لَهُ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ كَانَ زَاهِداً وَ كَانَ مِنْ أَعْبَدِ أَهْلِ زَمَانِهِ وَ كَانَ يَتَّقِيهِ السُّلْطَانُ لِجِدِّهِ فِي الدِّينِ وَ اجْتِهَادِهِ وَ رُبَّمَا اسْتَقْبَلَ السُّلْطَانَ بِكَلَامٍ صَعْبٍ يَعِظُهُ وَ يَأْمُرُهُ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَ يَنْهَاهُ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَ كَانَ السُّلْطَانُ يَحْتَمِلُهُ لِصَلَاحِهِ وَ لَمْ تَزَلْ هَذِهِ حَالَتَهُ حَتَّى كَانَ يَوْمٌ مِنَ الْأَيَّامِ إِذْ دَخَلَ عَلَيْهِ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ مُوسَى ( عليه السلام ) وَ هُوَ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ

فَرَآهُ فَأَوْمَأَ إِلَيْهِ فَأَتَاهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ يَا أَبَا عَلِيٍّ مَا أَحَبَّ إِلَيَّ مَا أَنْتَ فِيهِ وَ أَسَرَّنِي إِلَّا أَنَّهُ لَيْسَتْ لَكَ مَعْرِفَةٌ فَاطْلُبِ الْمَعْرِفَةَ قَالَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ وَ مَا الْمَعْرِفَةُ قَالَ اذْهَبْ فَتَفَقَّهْ وَ اطْلُبِ الْحَدِيثَ قَالَ عَمَّنْ قَالَ عَنْ فُقَهَاءِ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ ثُمَّ اعْرِضْ عَلَيَّ الْحَدِيثَ

قَالَ فَذَهَبَ فَكَتَبَ ثُمَّ جَاءَهُ فَقَرَأَهُ عَلَيْهِ فَأَسْقَطَهُ كُلَّهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ اذْهَبْ فَاعْرِفِ الْمَعْرِفَةَ وَ كَانَ الرَّجُلُ مَعْنِيّاً بِدِينِهِ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ يَتَرَصَّدُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) حَتَّى خَرَجَ إِلَى ضَيْعَةٍ لَهُ فَلَقِيَهُ فِي الطَّرِيقِ فَقَالَ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ إِنِّي أَحْتَجُّ عَلَيْكَ بَيْنَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ فَدُلَّنِي عَلَى الْمَعْرِفَةِ

قَالَ فَأَخْبَرَهُ بِأَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) وَ مَا كَانَ بَعْدَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ أَخْبَرَهُ بِأَمْرِ الرَّجُلَيْنِ فَقَبِلَ مِنْهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ فَمَنْ كَانَ بَعْدَ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ الْحَسَنُ ( عليه السلام ) ثُمَّ الْحُسَيْنُ ( عليه السلام ) حَتَّى انْتَهَى إِلَى نَفْسِهِ ثُمَّ سَكَتَ قَالَ فَقَالَ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ فَمَنْ هُوَ الْيَوْمَ قَالَ إِنْ أَخْبَرْتُكَ تَقْبَلُ قَالَ بَلَى جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ أَنَا هُوَ

قَالَ فَشَيْ‏ءٌ أَسْتَدِلُّ بِهِ قَالَ اذْهَبْ إِلَى تِلْكَ الشَّجَرَةِ وَ أَشَارَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى أُمِّ غَيْلَانَ فَقُلْ لَهَا يَقُولُ لَكِ مُوسَى بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ أَقْبِلِي قَالَ فَأَتَيْتُهَا فَرَأَيْتُهَا وَ اللَّهِ تَخُدُّ الْأَرْضَ خَدّاً حَتَّى وَقَفَتْ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ ثُمَّ أَشَارَ إِلَيْهَا فَرَجَعَتْ قَالَ فَأَقَرَّ بِهِ ثُمَّ لَزِمَ الصَّمْتَ وَ الْعِبَادَةَ فَكَانَ لَا يَرَاهُ أَحَدٌ يَتَكَلَّمُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ .
مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى وَ أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِيمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ مِثْلَهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Fulan the Waqifiite who said,

‘There was a cousin of mine called Al-Hassan Bin Abdullah who was ascetic, and he was from the most devout worshippers of his time, and the ruler used to fear him due to his devoutness in the Religion and his striving, and sometimes he used to face the ruler with harsh speech advising him and instructing him with the goodness and forbidding him from the evil, and it was so that the ruler used to endure him due to his righteousness. And this did not cease to be his state until it was from the days when he went over to Abu Al-Hassan Musa<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> was in the Masjid.

So he<sup>asws</sup> saw him and gestured towards him and said to him: ‘O Abu Ali! How beloved it is to me<sup>asws</sup> what you are in and it cheers me<sup>asws</sup> except that there isn’t any ‘مَعْرِفَة’ understanding for you. Therefore, seek the understanding’. He said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! And what is the understanding?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Go and ponder and seek the Hadeeth’. He said, ‘From whom?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘عَنْ فُقَهَاءِ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ’ ‘From the jurists of the people of Al Medina, then present the Hadeeth unto me<sup>asws</sup>’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘So he went, and wrote down (the Ahadeeth), then came over with it and recited it to him<sup>asws</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> rejected all of it, then said to him: ‘Go and recognise the understanding’. And he was the man serious with his Religion, so he did not cease to hang around Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> until he<sup>asws</sup> went out to a property of his<sup>asws</sup>, so he met him<sup>asws</sup> in the road. So he said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! I want to argue against you<sup>asws</sup> in front of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, so indicate me upon ‘الْمَعْرِفَةِ’ the recognition’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘So he<sup>asws</sup> informed him of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> and what had occurred after Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and informed him of the matter of the two men (Abu Bakr and Umar). So he accepted from him<sup>asws</sup>, then said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘So who was after Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, then Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>’, until he<sup>asws</sup> ended up to himself<sup>asws</sup>. Then he<sup>asws</sup> was silent. So he said, ‘So who is he (the Imam<sup>asws</sup>) today?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If I<sup>asws</sup> inform you, will you accept?’ He Said, ‘Yes, may I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>!’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> am he<sup>asws</sup>’.

He said: ‘So is there anything to evidence with it?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Go to that tree’, and he<sup>asws</sup> gestured towards Umm Gaylan (a type of tree), ‘and say to it, ‘Musa<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> is telling you, ‘come to me’. He said, ‘So I went over to it and said it. By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, it furrowed the ground with a furrowing until it stopped in front of him<sup>asws</sup>. Then he<sup>asws</sup> gestured towards it, so it returned. So he acknowledged with it, the necessitated the silence and the worship (upon himself), and it was so that no one saw him speak after that’.
Muhammad Bin Yahya, and Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Ibrahim Bin Hashim – similar to it.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Muhammad from Muhammad ibn so and so al-Waqifi ( a certain sect) who has said the following. “I had a son of uncle. He wascalled al-Hassan ibn ‘Abdallah He was Zahid (restricted himself from worldly pleasures). He was the foremost in worship of his time. He king would also observe cautions due to his assiduousness and seriousness in the matters of religion. He would even demand the king to respect and maintain the lawful and the unlawful rule with strictness. The king would bear with him due to his virtuousness. It continued as such every day until one day abu al-Hassan (a.s.) went to him while he was in the mosque and called him with a hand gesture. When he came near abu al-Hassan (a.s.). The Imam (a.s.) said, “O abu Ali, I like your practice very much and it makes me happy. However, you do not have any understanding. You must seek understanding.” He said, “May Allah take my soul in service for your cause, what is understanding?” he said, “Go and try to understand and learn hadith.” He then asked, “From who must I learn hadith?” The Imam (a.s.) said, ‘Learn from the law specialist of Madina. Then read them before me to check.” The narrator has said that he went, wrote (some hadith), came back and read them before him. The Imam (a.s.) deleted all of them and said to him go and learn how to understand.” The man was very serious in his religion. He kept watching abu al-Hassan (a.s.) until one day he went out to find something that was missing and the man met him on the way. He said, May Allah take my soul in service for your cause, I will debate you before Allah unless you teach me how to have understanding.” The Imam (a.s.) explained to him about Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.) and had happened after the the Messenger of Allah. He also explained to him about the two men and he agreed. Then he asked, “who was (Leadership with Divine Authority) after Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.)?” He said, “al-Hassan (a.s.) was the (Leadership with Divine Authority), then al-Husayn (a.s.). He mentioned (the Imams one after the other) up to his own self and remained silent.” The narrator has said that the man then asked, “May Allah take my soul in service for your cause, who is (Leadership with Divine Authority) today?” The Imam (a.s.) said, “were I to tell you would then accept?” he said, “May Allah take my soul in service for your cause, yes, I will accept.” He sad, “I am the one (Leadership with Divine Authority).” He then said, “Is there any evidence I can use as proof?” The Imam (a.s.) said, “Go to that tree. He pointed out with his hand to ’Umm Ghaylan and say to it, “Musa ibn Ja’far says, ‘Come to me.” He has said that I then went to the tree and saw it, by Allah, it cutting the earth a real cut until it stood before him. Then he made a gesture and it returned.” The narrator has said that he acknowledged the his Imamat (Leadership with Divine Authority). He remained silent and devoted himself in worship and no one thereafter saw him speak.” Muhammad ibn Yahya and Ahmad ibn Muhammad have narrated from Mutammad ibn al-Hassan from Ibrahim ibn Hashim a similar hadith.

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول أو ضعيف بأبي يحي ى - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/99)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: سَمِعْتُ يَحْيَى بْنَ أَكْثَمَ قَاضِيَ سَامَرَّاءَ بَعْدَ مَا جَهَدْتُ بِهِ وَ نَاظَرْتُهُ وَ حَاوَرْتُهُ وَ وَاصَلْتُهُ وَ سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ عُلُومِ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ فَقَالَ بَيْنَا أَنَا ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ دَخَلْتُ أَطُوفُ بِقَبْرِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَرَأَيْتُ مُحَمَّدَ بْنَ عَلِيٍّ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) يَطُوفُ بِهِ

فَنَاظَرْتُهُ فِي مَسَائِلَ عِنْدِي فَأَخْرَجَهَا إِلَيَّ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ وَ اللَّهِ إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ أَسْأَلَكَ مَسْأَلَةً وَ إِنِّي وَ اللَّهِ لَأَسْتَحْيِي مِنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ لِي أَنَا أُخْبِرُكَ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَسْأَلَنِي تَسْأَلُنِي عَنِ الْإِمَامِ فَقُلْتُ هُوَ وَ اللَّهِ هَذَا فَقَالَ أَنَا هُوَ فَقُلْتُ عَلَامَةً فَكَانَ فِي يَدِهِ عَصًا فَنَطَقَتْ وَ قَالَتْ إِنَّ مَوْلَايَ إِمَامُ هَذَا الزَّمَانِ وَ هُوَ الْحُجَّةُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, and Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Ahmad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Al Tayyib, from Abdul Wahhab Bin Mansour, from Muhammad Bin Abu Al A’ala who said,

‘I heard Yahya Bin Aksam, the judge of Samarra, after having tested him, and debated him, and interviewed him, and maintaining good relations with him, and I asked him about the knowledge of the Progeny<sup>asws</sup> of Muhammad<sup>saww</sup>. So he said, ‘One day when I entered to perform Tawaaf of the grave of Rasol-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, so I saw Muhammad<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> performing Tawaaf of it.

So I debated with him<sup>asws</sup> regarding the questions which were with me, so he extracted (the answers) to me. So I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I want to ask you<sup>asws</sup> a question and I, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, am too embarrassed from that’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> shall inform you before you even ask me<sup>asws</sup>. You want to ask me<sup>asws</sup> about the Imam<sup>asws</sup>’. So I said, ‘By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! This is it’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> am he<sup>asws</sup>’. So I said, ‘It’s sign?’ So there was a staff in his<sup>asws</sup> hand, and it spoke and said, ‘My owner is the Imam<sup>asws</sup> of this time, and he<sup>asws</sup> is the Divine Authority’.
en.sarwar: 9. Muhammad ibn Yahya and Ahmad ibn Muhammad have narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan from Ahmad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn at-Tayyib from ‘Abd al-Wahhab ibn Mansur from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-‘Ala’ who has said the following. “I heard from Yahya ibn Aktham, the judge in the city of Samarra, Iraq his words herein below. It happened only after serious debates, conversations, maintaining good relations and asking him about the knowledge of the members of the family of Muhammad (s.a). He said, “Once when was walking around the grave of the Messenger of Allah I saw Muhammad ibn Ali al-Rida (a.s.) also walk around the grave of the Messenger of Allah. We debated over the issues that I needed to understand. He explained them to me. I then said, “By Allah, I want to ask you a question, but I swear by Allah that I feel shy.” He then said, “I can tell you what you want to ask before you will ask me about it. You want to ask me about Imamat (Leadership with Divine Authority).” I then said, “By Allah, this is it?” He said, “I am he.” I then asked, “Is there any evidence and proof to support it?” He had a staff in his hand and it spoke, “My master certainly is the Imam of this time and he is the Hujja (Leader with Divine Authority)>“

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/100)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: صحيح - Sahih al-Kafi (41/ 1)
text: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) وَ أَنَا يَوْمَئِذٍ وَاقِفٌ وَ قَدْ كَانَ أَبِي سَأَلَ أَبَاهُ عَنْ سَبْعِ مَسَائِلَ فَأَجَابَهُ فِي سِتٍّ وَ أَمْسَكَ عَنِ السَّابِعَةِ فَقُلْتُ وَ اللَّهِ لَأَسْأَلَنَّهُ عَمَّا سَأَلَ أَبِي أَبَاهُ فَإِنْ أَجَابَ بِمِثْلِ جَوَابِ أَبِيهِ كَانَتْ دَلَالَةً فَسَأَلْتُهُ فَأَجَابَ بِمِثْلِ جَوَابِ أَبِيهِ أَبِي فِي الْمَسَائِلِ السِّتِّ فَلَمْ يَزِدْ فِي الْجَوَابِ وَاواً وَ لَا يَاءً وَ أَمْسَكَ عَنِ السَّابِعَةِ

وَ قَدْ كَانَ أَبِي قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ إِنِّي أَحْتَجُّ عَلَيْكَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ أَنَّكَ زَعَمْتَ أَنَّ عَبْدَ اللَّهِ لَمْ يَكُنْ إِمَاماً فَوَضَعَ يَدَهُ عَلَى عُنُقِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُ نَعَمْ احْتَجَّ عَلَيَّ بِذَلِكَ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَمَا كَانَ فِيهِ مِنْ إِثْمٍ فَهُوَ فِي رَقَبَتِي

فَلَمَّا وَدَّعْتُهُ قَالَ إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ أَحَدٌ مِنْ شِيعَتِنَا يُبْتَلَى بِبَلِيَّةٍ أَوْ يَشْتَكِي فَيَصْبِرُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ إِلَّا كَتَبَ اللَّهُ لَهُ أَجْرَ أَلْفِ شَهِيدٍ فَقُلْتُ فِي نَفْسِي وَ اللَّهِ مَا كَانَ لِهَذَا ذِكْرٌ

فَلَمَّا مَضَيْتُ وَ كُنْتُ فِي بَعْضِ الطَّرِيقِ خَرَجَ بِي عِرْقُ الْمَدِينِيِّ فَلَقِيتُ مِنْهُ شِدَّةً فَلَمَّا كَانَ مِنْ قَابِلٍ حَجَجْتُ فَدَخَلْتُ عَلَيْهِ وَ قَدْ بَقِيَ مِنْ وَجَعِي بَقِيَّةٌ فَشَكَوْتُ إِلَيْهِ وَ قُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ عَوِّذْ رِجْلِي وَ بَسَطْتُهَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَقَالَ لِي لَيْسَ عَلَى رِجْلِكَ هَذِهِ بَأْسٌ وَ لَكِنْ أَرِنِي رِجْلَكَ الصَّحِيحَةَ فَبَسَطْتُهَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ فَعَوَّذَهَا فَلَمَّا خَرَجْتُ لَمْ أَلْبَثْ إِلَّا يَسِيراً حَتَّى خَرَجَ بِيَ الْعِرْقُ وَ كَانَ وَجَعُهُ يَسِيراً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, or someone else, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Al Husayn Bin Umar Bin Yazeed who said,

‘I went over to Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, and I, in those days, was a Waqifiite, and it was so that my father had asked his<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> about seven questions and he<sup>asws</sup> hand answered six and withheld from the seventh. So I said (to myself), ‘By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I shall ask him<sup>asws</sup> about what my father asked his<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>. So if he<sup>asws</sup> were to answer with the like of the answer of his<sup>asws</sup> father, it would be proof’. So I asked him<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> answered with like the answer of his<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> to my father regarding the six questions, and he<sup>asws</sup> did not increase in the answer with neither a (letter) ‘Waw’ nor a (letter) ‘Ya’, and he<sup>asws</sup> withheld from the seventh.

And my father had said to his<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>, ‘I shall argue against you<sup>asws</sup> in the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> on the Day of Judgment. You<sup>asws</sup> are claiming that Abdullah does not happen to be the Imam’. So he<sup>asws</sup> had placed his<sup>asws</sup> hand upon his neck, then said to him: ‘Yes, argue against me<sup>asws</sup> with that on the Day of Judgment in the Presence of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic. So whatever was in it from a sin, so it would be upon my<sup>asws</sup> neck.

So when I bade him<sup>asws</sup> farewell, he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘There isn’t anyone from our<sup>asws</sup> Shias who is afflicted with an affliction or a complaint, so he is patient upon that, except that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Write to him the Recompense of a thousand martyrs’. So I said within myself, ‘By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! There was no mention of this’.

So when I went and was in one of the streets, a leg vein came out with me (swelled) and I faced intense (pain) from it. So when it was from the next year, I performed Hajj, and I went over to him<sup>asws</sup>, and there had (still) remained some pain from my pains. So I complained to him<sup>asws</sup>, and I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! My leg is hurting’ and I extended it in front of him<sup>asws</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘There is nothing wrong upon this leg of yours, but show me<sup>asws</sup> your correct leg’. So I extended it in front of him<sup>asws</sup> and he<sup>asws</sup> sought Refuge (with Allah<sup>azwj</sup>) for it. So when I left, I did not wait except for a little while until the vein swelled with me, and it was of a little pain’.
en.sarwar: 10. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, or a person other than him from Ali ibn al-Hakam from al-Husayn ibn ‘Umar ibn Yazid who has said the following. “Once I went to see Ali al-Rida (a.s.). At that time I was a memebr of Waqifi sect. My father had asked his (al-Rida’s) father seven questions. He had replied to six of them and had refrained from answering the seventh. I said to my self, “By Allah, I will ask him what my father had asked his father if his answers will like those of his father it will serve as a good proof. I asked him those questions and hiis answers were just like the answers of his father to my father in the six questions. He did answer any more questions beyond six even not a letter ‘waw’ or letter ‘ya’’ and refrained from answering the seventh question. My father had said to his father, “I will debate you before Allah on the Day of Judgment for your belief that ‘Abdallah is not an Imam. He then had placed his hand over my father’s neck and said, ““Yes, you may debate me about it before Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most gracious. If there will any sins in it will be on my neck.” When I said farewell to him he said, “Whoever of our Shi‘a would suffer from any kind of afflictions or complains and he bears it patiently, Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most gracious, will record for an entitlement to a reward equal to the rewards of a thousand martyrs.” I then said to my self, “By Allah, there was no mention of this (in our conversations).” I then left and on the way a vein in my began to feel very soar and the pain intensified. Next year when I went for Hajj I went to see the him. My leg was still slightly painful. I complained before him of the pain and requested him to say a prayer on it and I stretched it before him.” He said, “There is nothing wrong with this leg but show me your leg that is not ill. I then stretched it before him and he said a prayer. When I left little later on my leg began to feel the pain in a vein but it was very little.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/101)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: دَخَلْتُ عَلَى عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُوسَى الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) فَقُلْتُ لَهُ يَكُونُ إِمَامَانِ قَالَ لَا إِلَّا وَ أَحَدُهُمَا صَامِتٌ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ هُوَ ذَا أَنْتَ لَيْسَ لَكَ صَامِتٌ وَ لَمْ يَكُنْ وُلِدَ لَهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ بَعْدُ فَقَالَ لِي وَ اللَّهِ لَيَجْعَلَنَّ اللَّهُ مِنِّي مَا يُثْبِتُ بِهِ الْحَقَّ وَ أَهْلَهُ وَ يَمْحَقُ بِهِ الْبَاطِلَ وَ أَهْلَهُ

فَوُلِدَ لَهُ بَعْدَ سَنَةٍ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) فَقِيلَ لِابْنِ قِيَامَا أَ لَا تُقْنِعُكَ هَذِهِ الْآيَةُ فَقَالَ أَمَا وَ اللَّهِ إِنَّهَا لآَيَةٌ عَظِيمَةٌ وَ لَكِنْ كَيْفَ أَصْنَعُ بِمَا قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي ابْنِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Mihran, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Ibn Qiyama Al Wasity, and he was from the Waqifiites who said,

‘I went over to Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Musa Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>, and I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Can there happen to be two Imams<sup>asws</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, except that one of the two would be silent’. So I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘It is that. You<sup>asws</sup> are such that there isn’t a silent one for you<sup>asws</sup>’. And there had not happened the birth of Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> for him<sup>asws</sup> yet. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would be Making from me<sup>asws</sup> what the Truth and its people would be affirmed by and the falsehood and its people would be obliterated by’.

So there came for him<sup>asws</sup>, after a year, Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>. So it was said to Ibn Qiyama (the narrator), ‘Does this sign not suffice for you?’ So he said, ‘But, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, it is a great sign, but how shall I deal with what Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said regarding his<sup>asws</sup> son?’
en.sarwar: 11. Ahmad ibn Mihran has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ali from ibn Qiyama al-Wasiti who was a member of the waqifa, sect has said the following. “Once I went to see Ali ibn Musa al-Rida (a.s.) and I said to him, “Can there be two Imams (at the same time)?” He said, “No, except if one of them would be silent.” I then said, “What about your self? There is no silent Imam with you.” His son, abu Ja’far (a.s.) was not yet born. He said, “By Allah, Allah will create from me someone to establish the truth and the people of truth and banish falsehood and the people of falsehood.” After a year therefrom abu Ja’far (a.s.) was born. It was said to ibn Qiyama, “Does this sign not convince you?” He then said, “By Allah, the sign is great but what would I say about the words of abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) about his son.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/102)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: أَتَيْتُ خُرَاسَانَ وَ أَنَا وَاقِفٌ فَحَمَلْتُ مَعِي مَتَاعاً وَ كَانَ مَعِي ثَوْبٌ وَشِيٌّ فِي بَعْضِ الرِّزَمِ وَ لَمْ أَشْعُرْ بِهِ وَ لَمْ أَعْرِفْ مَكَانَهُ فَلَمَّا قَدِمْتُ مَرْوَ وَ نَزَلْتُ فِي بَعْضِ مَنَازِلِهَا لَمْ أَشْعُرْ إِلَّا وَ رَجُلٌ مَدَنِيٌّ مِنْ بَعْضِ مُوَلَّدِيهَا فَقَالَ لِي إِنَّ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ لَكَ ابْعَثْ إِلَيَّ الثَّوْبَ الْوَشِيَّ الَّذِي عِنْدَكَ قَالَ فَقُلْتُ وَ مَنْ أَخْبَرَ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ بِقُدُومِي وَ أَنَا قَدِمْتُ آنِفاً وَ مَا عِنْدِي ثَوْبٌ وَشِيٌّ

فَرَجَعَ إِلَيْهِ وَ عَادَ إِلَيَّ فَقَالَ يَقُولُ لَكَ بَلَى هُوَ فِي مَوْضِعِ كَذَا وَ كَذَا وَ رِزْمَتُهُ كَذَا وَ كَذَا فَطَلَبْتُهُ حَيْثُ قَالَ فَوَجَدْتُهُ فِي أَسْفَلِ الرِّزْمَةِ فَبَعَثْتُ بِهِ إِلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa who said,

‘I went to Khurasan and I was a Waqifiite. So I carried provisions and there were clothes for me and something in one of the packets, and I was not aware of it and did not know of its place. So when I proceeded to Merv and lodged in one of its lodgings and did not publicise except that a Medinite man from one of its tribes came over and said to me, ‘Abu Al-Hassan Al-Rezaasws is saying to you: ‘Send over to measws the Washy cloth which is with you’. So I said, ‘And who informed Abu Al-Hassanasws of my coming over here and I have just come, and there is not Washy cloth with me’.

So he returned to him<sup>asws</sup> and came back to me and said, ‘He<sup>asws</sup> is saying to you: ‘Yes (you do), and it is in such and such a place, and in such and such a packet’. So I looked for it where he<sup>asws</sup> said it to be, and I found it in the lowest of the packets. So I sent it over to him<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 12. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla ibn Muhammad from (al-Hassan ibn Ali) al-Washsha’ who has said the following. “I came to Khurasan when I was a member of the Waqifa sect. I had certain goods with me. In these goods there was a printed piece of fabric in one of the bundles. It was misplaced and did not know where it was. When I arrived in Marv and found a lodging a man who was born in the city of Madina came by and said to me, “Abu al-Hassan al-Rida (a.s.) said to you, “send to me the piece of printed fabric that is with you.” He has said that I then said, “Who has told abu al-Hassan (a.s.) about my arrival here? I have just arrived. There is no printed fabric with me.” He went back to the Imam (a.s.) and returned to me saying, “The Imam (a.s.) says, “It is in such and such place and in such and such bundle.” I then looked for it as he had said and found it at the bottom of the bundle and then I sent it to him.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق وفي أول السند إرسا ل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (103/ 4)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: صحيح - Sahih al-Kafi (1/41)
text: كُنْتُ وَاقِفاً وَ حَجَجْتُ عَلَى تِلْكَ الْحَالِ فَلَمَّا صِرْتُ بِمَكَّةَ خَلَجَ فِي صَدْرِي شَيْ‏ءٌ فَتَعَلَّقْتُ بِالْمُلْتَزَمِ ثُمَّ قُلْتُ اللَّهُمَّ قَدْ عَلِمْتَ طَلِبَتِي وَ إِرَادَتِي فَأَرْشِدْنِي إِلَى خَيْرِ الْأَدْيَانِ فَوَقَعَ فِي نَفْسِي أَنْ آتِيَ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام )

فَأَتَيْتُ الْمَدِينَةَ فَوَقَفْتُ بِبَابِهِ وَ قُلْتُ لِلْغُلَامِ قُلْ لِمَوْلَاكَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْعِرَاقِ بِالْبَابِ قَالَ فَسَمِعْتُ نِدَاءَهُ وَ هُوَ يَقُولُ ادْخُلْ يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ الْمُغِيرَةِ ادْخُلْ يَا عَبْدَ اللَّهِ بْنَ الْمُغِيرَةِ فَدَخَلْتُ فَلَمَّا نَظَرَ إِلَيَّ قَالَ لِي قَدْ أَجَابَ اللَّهُ دُعَاءَكَ وَ هَدَاكَ لِدِينِهِ فَقُلْتُ أَشْهَدُ أَنَّكَ حُجَّةُ اللَّهِ وَ أَمِينُهُ عَلَى خَلْقِهِ .

en.hubeali: Ibn Fazzal, from Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira who said,

‘I was Waqifiite and I performed Hajj being upon that state. So when I came to Makkah, something disturbed me in my chest, so I attached with Al-Multazim (a wall of the Kaaba), then I said, ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! You<sup>azwj</sup> have Known of my seeking and my intention, therefore Guide me to the best of the Religions’. So there occurred within myself that I should go to Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup>.

So I went over to Al-Medina and paused at his<sup>asws</sup> door and said to the slave, ‘Tell your Master<sup>asws</sup> that a man from the people of Al-Iraq is at the door’. So I heard his<sup>asws</sup> call and he<sup>asws</sup> was saying: ‘Enter, O Abdullah Bin Al-Mugheira! Enter, O Abdullah Bin Al Mugheira!’ So I entered. So when he<sup>asws</sup> looked at me, he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> has Answered your supplication and Guided you to His<sup>azwj</sup> Religion’. So I said, ‘I testify that you<sup>asws</sup> are the Divine Authority of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Trustee upon His<sup>azwj</sup> creatures’.
en.sarwar: 13. Ibn Faddall has narrated from ‘Abdallah ibn al-Mughira who has said the following. “I was a member of Waqifa sect and I wen to Hajj with the same belief. When I arrived in Makka an idea began to prick my chest. I got hold of al-Multazam (the wall next to the door of Ka‘ba) and said, “O Lord, You know what I ask for and my intention. Guide me to the best of the religions. It then occurred to me to see al-Rida (a.s.). I went to the city of Madina and stood in front of his door and said to his slave, ‘Tell your master that a man from Iraq is at the door.’” He has said that I heard his voice saying, “O ‘Abdallah ibn Mughira, come in.” I then went inside and when he looked at me he said, “Allah has answered your prayer and has guided you to His religion.” I then said, “I testify that you possess Divine Authority and you are the trustee of Allah over His creatures.” :

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/104)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

text: كَانَ عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ هُلَيْلٍ يَقُولُ بِعَبْدِ اللَّهِ فَصَارَ إِلَى الْعَسْكَرِ فَرَجَعَ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَسَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ سَبَبِ رُجُوعِهِ فَقَالَ إِنِّي عَرَضْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنْ أَسْأَلَهُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَوَافَقَنِي فِي طَرِيقٍ ضَيِّقٍ فَمَالَ نَحْوِي حَتَّى إِذَا حَاذَانِي أَقْبَلَ نَحْوِي بِشَيْ‏ءٍ مِنْ فِيهِ فَوَقَعَ عَلَى صَدْرِي فَأَخَذْتُهُ فَإِذَا هُوَ رَقٌّ فِيهِ مَكْتُوبٌ مَا كَانَ هُنَالِكَ وَ لَا كَذَلِكَ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abdullah who said,

‘It was so that Abdullah Bin Huleyl was saying that Abdullah (son of Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> as being the Imam). So he went to Al-Askar and retracted from that. So I asked him about the reason for his retraction. So he said, ‘I went to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> to ask him<sup>asws</sup> about that, and he<sup>asws</sup> was with me in one of the narrow streets. So when he<sup>asws</sup> was besides me, when he<sup>asws</sup> threw something towards me from his<sup>asws</sup> mouth. So it landed upon my chest, and I grabbed it, and it was a parchment in which was written: ‘It (Imamate) was not there nor was it like that’.
en.sarwar: 14. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla ibn Muhammad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Abdallah who has said the following. “‘Abdallah ibn Hulayl believe in ‘Abdallah (ibn Ja’far (a.s.) ) to be the Imam). He went to the army head quarters in Samarra, Iraq and thereafter he changed his belief. I asked him about the reason for such change. He said, “I thought (while in Samarra) I should discuss it with abu al-Hassan (a.s.) and ask him about it. Accidentally I came across him when passing through a narrow alley. He came closer and when parallel to me, he spitted out on me something from his mouth and it was placed on my chest. I then picked it up. It was a leaf with a writing on it that said, “He was not there (position of Leadership with Divine Authority) and he was not qualified for it (Leadership with Divine Authority).”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (105/ 4)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ قَالَ أَخْبَرَنَا مُوسَى بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ عُبَيْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْعَبَّاسِ بْنِ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِي جَعْفَرُ بْنُ زَيْدِ بْنِ مُوسَى عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ( عليهم السلام ) قَالُوا جَاءَتْ أُمُّ أَسْلَمَ يَوْماً إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ هُوَ فِي مَنْزِلِ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ فَسَأَلَتْهَا عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَتْ خَرَجَ فِي بَعْضِ الْحَوَائِجِ وَ السَّاعَةَ يَجِي‏ءُ

فَانْتَظَرَتْهُ عِنْدَ أُمِّ سَلَمَةَ حَتَّى جَاءَ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَتْ أُمُّ أَسْلَمَ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَ أُمِّي يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ إِنِّي قَدْ قَرَأْتُ الْكُتُبَ وَ عَلِمْتُ كُلَّ نَبِيٍّ وَ وَصِيٍّ فَمُوسَى كَانَ لَهُ وَصِيٌّ فِي حَيَاتِهِ وَ وَصِيٌّ بَعْدَ مَوْتِهِ وَ كَذَلِكَ عِيسَى فَمَنْ وَصِيُّكَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَقَالَ لَهَا يَا أُمَّ أَسْلَمَ وَصِيِّي فِي حَيَاتِي وَ بَعْدَ مَمَاتِي وَاحِدٌ

ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهَا يَا أُمَّ أَسْلَمَ مَنْ فَعَلَ فِعْلِي هَذَا فَهُوَ وَصِيِّي ثُمَّ ضَرَبَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى حَصَاةٍ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ فَفَرَكَهَا بِإِصْبَعِهِ فَجَعَلَهَا شِبْهَ الدَّقِيقِ ثُمَّ عَجَنَهَا ثُمَّ طَبَعَهَا بِخَاتَمِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ مَنْ فَعَلَ فِعْلِي هَذَا فَهُوَ وَصِيِّي فِي حَيَاتِي وَ بَعْدَ مَمَاتِي

فَخَرَجْتُ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ فَأَتَيْتُ أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فَقُلْتُ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَ أُمِّي أَنْتَ وَصِيُّ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) قَالَ نَعَمْ يَا أُمَّ أَسْلَمَ ثُمَّ ضَرَبَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى حَصَاةٍ فَفَرَكَهَا فَجَعَلَهَا كَهَيْئَةِ الدَّقِيقِ ثُمَّ عَجَنَهَا وَ خَتَمَهَا بِخَاتَمِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا أُمَّ أَسْلَمَ مَنْ فَعَلَ فِعْلِي هَذَا فَهُوَ وَصِيِّي فَخَرَجْتُ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ فَأَتَيْتُ أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فَقُلْتُ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَ أُمِّي أَنْتَ وَصِيُّ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) قَالَ نَعَمْ يَا أُمَّ أَسْلَمَ ثُمَّ ضَرَبَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى حَصَاةٍ فَفَرَكَهَا فَجَعَلَهَا كَهَيْئَةِ الدَّقِيقِ ثُمَّ عَجَنَهَا وَ خَتَمَهَا بِخَاتَمِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ يَا أُمَّ أَسْلَمَ مَنْ فَعَلَ فِعْلِي هَذَا فَهُوَ وَصِيِّي

فَأَتَيْتُ الْحَسَنَ ( عليه السلام ) وَ هُوَ غُلَامٌ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ يَا سَيِّدِي أَنْتَ وَصِيُّ أَبِيكَ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ يَا أُمَّ أَسْلَمَ وَ ضَرَبَ بِيَدِهِ وَ أَخَذَ حَصَاةً فَفَعَلَ بِهَا كَفِعْلِهِمَا

فَخَرَجْتُ مِنْ عِنْدِهِ فَأَتَيْتُ الْحُسَيْنَ ( عليه السلام ) وَ إِنِّي لَمُسْتَصْغِرَةٌ لِسِنِّهِ فَقُلْتُ لَهُ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَ أُمِّي أَنْتَ وَصِيُّ أَخِيكَ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ يَا أُمَّ أَسْلَمَ ائْتِينِي بِحَصَاةٍ ثُمَّ فَعَلَ كَفِعْلِهِمْ

فَعَمَرَتْ أُمُّ أَسْلَمَ حَتَّى لَحِقَتْ بِعَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ بَعْدَ قَتْلِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي مُنْصَرَفِهِ فَسَأَلَتْهُ أَنْتَ وَصِيُّ أَبِيكَ فَقَالَ نَعَمْ ثُمَّ فَعَلَ كَفِعْلِهِمْ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِمْ أَجْمَعِينَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad, from one of our companions whose name he mentioned, said, ‘Muhammad Bin Ibrahim narrated to us saying,

‘Musa Bin Muhammad Bin Ismail Bin Ubeydullah son of Al-Abbas<sup>asws</sup> Bin Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Abu Talib<sup>asws</sup> informed us saying, Ja’far Bin Zayd son of Musa<sup>asws</sup>, from his<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>, from his<sup>asws</sup> forefathers<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘One day Umm Aslam came over to the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> was in the house of Umm Salma<sup>as</sup>. So she asked her<sup>as</sup> about Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>. So she<sup>as</sup> said: ‘He<sup>saww</sup> went out regarding some need and he<sup>saww</sup> would be coming back after a while’.

So she waited in the presence of Umm Salma<sup>as</sup> until he<sup>saww</sup> came over. So Umm Aslam said, ‘May my father and my mother be (sacrificed) for you<sup>saww</sup>, O Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>! I have read in books and I know every Prophet<sup>as</sup> and successor<sup>as</sup>. So Musa<sup>as</sup>, there was a successor<sup>as</sup> for him<sup>as</sup> during his<sup>as</sup> lifetime and after his<sup>as</sup> death. And similar to that was Isa<sup>as</sup>. So who is your<sup>saww</sup> successor<sup>asws</sup>, O Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>?’ So he<sup>saww</sup> said to her: ‘O Umm Aslam! My<sup>saww</sup> successor<sup>asws</sup> during my<sup>saww</sup> lifetime and after my<sup>saww</sup> passing away is one’.

Then he<sup>saww</sup> said to her: ‘O Umm Aslam! The one who does this deed of mine<sup>saww</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> is my<sup>saww</sup> successor<sup>asws</sup>’. Then he<sup>saww</sup> struck his<sup>saww</sup> hand upon a pebble from the ground and crushed it by his<sup>saww</sup> fingers and made it be like flour, then kneaded it, then imprinted by his<sup>saww</sup> seal, then said: ‘The one who does this deed of mine<sup>saww</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> is my<sup>saww</sup> successor<sup>asws</sup> during my<sup>saww</sup> lifetime and after my<sup>saww</sup> passing away’.

(So she said), ‘I exited from his<sup>saww</sup> presence and went over to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> and I said, ‘May my father and my mother be (sacrificed) for you<sup>asws</sup>! Are you<sup>asws</sup> the successor<sup>asws</sup> of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, O Umm Aslam! Then he<sup>asws</sup> struck his<sup>asws</sup> hand upon a pebble, and he<sup>asws</sup> crushed as if it was flour, then kneaded it, and sealed it by his<sup>asws</sup> insignia, then said: ‘O Umm Aslam! The one who does this deed of mine<sup>asws</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> is my<sup>asws</sup> successor<sup>asws</sup>’.

So I went over to Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, and he<sup>asws</sup> was a boy, and I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘O my Master<sup>asws</sup>! Are you a successor<sup>asws</sup> of your<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, O Umm Aslam!’ And he<sup>asws</sup> struck by his<sup>asws</sup> hand and grabbed a pebble and did with the like of both their<sup>asws</sup> deeds.

So I exited from his<sup>asws</sup> presence and I went over to Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>, and I considered him<sup>asws</sup> of a small age, so I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May my father and my mother be (sacrificed) for you<sup>asws</sup>! Are you a successor<sup>asws</sup> of your<sup>asws</sup> brother<sup>asws</sup>?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes, O Umm Aslam! Bring me a pebble’. Then he<sup>asws</sup> did the like of their<sup>asws</sup> deeds’.

So Umm Aslam lived until she met up with Aliasws Bin Al-Husaynasws after the martyrdom of Al-Husaynasws, upon hisasws return (from Al-Iraq). So she asked himasws, ‘Are youasws a successorasws of yourasws fatherasws?’ So heasws said: ‘Yes’. Then heasws did the like of theirasws deeds. May the Salawat of Allahazwj be upon all of themasws’.
en.sarwar: 15. Ali ibn Muhammad has narrated from one of our people whose name he mentioned. He said that Muhammad ibn Ibrahim narrated to us, that Musa ibn Muhammad ibn ’Isma‘il ibn ‘Ubaydallah ibn ‘Abbass ibn Ali ibn abu Talib narrated to us from Ja’far ibn Zayd ibn Musa from his father, from his forefathers the following. “One day ’Umm Aslam came to the Holy Prophet (s.a) when he was in the house of ’Umm salama. She asked about the Messenger of Allah. ’Umm Salama relied that he had just went out for some thing and that he would return soon. She then waited with ’Umm Salama until he (the Messenger of Allah) came. ’Umm Aslam then said, “May Allah take my soul and the souls of my parents in service for your cause, Messenger of Allah, I have read the books and have learned about all the prophets and the executors of the their will. Moses had an executor for his will even in his life time and an executor of the will after he died. So also was Jesus. Who then is the executor of your will, O the Messenger of Allah?” He then said to her, “O ’Umm Aslam, the executor of my will in my lifetime and after I will is the same person.” Then he said to her, “O ’Umm Aslam, “Whoever would be able to do what I intend to do just now he is and will be the executor of my will.” He then picked up a pebble from the floor and crushed it with his fingers into something like flour. He then turned it into a piece of clay and then printed his seal on it and said, “Whoever would be able to do what I just did he will be theexecutor of my will in my lifetime and after I will die.” She then left him. She has said that she went to Amir al-Mu’minin Ali (a.s.) and said, “May Allah take my soul and the souls of my parents in service for your cause, are you the executor orf the will of the Messenger of Allah?” He said, “Yes, I am, O ’Umm Aslam.” He then picked up a pebble, crushed it into powder like flour, turned it into clay and printed his seal on it. He then said, “O ’Umm Aslam, whoever would be able to do what I just did he will be the executor of my will.” I them went to al-Hassan (a.s.) while he was a young boy. I asked him, “My master, are you the executor of the will of yourfather?” He said, “Yes, I am, O ’Umm Aslam.” He picked up a pebble and did exactly what his father had done. I left him and went ot al-Husayn (a.s.) and considered him very little because of his young age. I asked him, “Are you the executor of the will of your brother?” He saaid, “Yes, I am. O ’Umm Aslam bring me a pebble.” He then did exactly what they had done. She has said that she lived until she met Ali ibn al-Husayn (a.s.) after the martyrdom of his father, on his return from Iraq. I asked him, “Are you the executor of the will of your father?” He said, “Yes, I am. He then also did exactly what they (a.s.) had done.”

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (111/ 4)
Shaykh Baqir al-Behbudi: ضعيف - Sahih al-Kafi
text: زَيْدَ بْنَ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) دَخَلَ عَلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِيٍّ وَ مَعَهُ كُتُبٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكُوفَةِ يَدْعُونَهُ فِيهَا إِلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ وَ يُخْبِرُونَهُ بِاجْتِمَاعِهِمْ وَ يَأْمُرُونَهُ بِالْخُرُوجِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) هَذِهِ الْكُتُبُ ابْتِدَاءٌ مِنْهُمْ أَوْ جَوَابُ مَا كَتَبْتَ بِهِ إِلَيْهِمْ وَ دَعَوْتَهُمْ إِلَيْهِ فَقَالَ بَلِ ابْتِدَاءٌ مِنَ الْقَوْمِ لِمَعْرِفَتِهِمْ بِحَقِّنَا وَ بِقَرَابَتِنَا مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ لِمَا يَجِدُونَ فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنْ وُجُوبِ مَوَدَّتِنَا وَ فَرْضِ طَاعَتِنَا وَ لِمَا نَحْنُ فِيهِ مِنَ الضِّيقِ وَ الضَّنْكِ وَ الْبَلَاءِ

فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّ الطَّاعَةَ مَفْرُوضَةٌ مِنَ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ سُنَّةٌ أَمْضَاهَا فِي الْأَوَّلِينَ وَ كَذَلِكَ يُجْرِيهَا فِي الْآخِرِينَ وَ الطَّاعَةُ لِوَاحِدٍ مِنَّا وَ الْمَوَدَّةُ لِلْجَمِيعِ وَ أَمْرُ اللَّهِ يَجْرِي لِأَوْلِيَائِهِ بِحُكْمٍ مَوْصُولٍ وَ قَضَاءٍ مَفْصُولٍ وَ حَتْمٍ مَقْضِيٍّ وَ قَدَرٍ مَقْدُورٍ وَ أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى لِوَقْتٍ مَعْلُومٍ فَلَا يَسْتَخِفَّنَّكَ الَّذِينَ لَا يُوقِنُونَ إِنَّهُمْ لَنْ يُغْنُوا عَنْكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيْئاً فَلَا تَعْجَلْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَعْجَلُ لِعَجَلَةِ الْعِبَادِ وَ لَا تَسْبِقَنَّ اللَّهَ فَتُعْجِزَكَ الْبَلِيَّةُ فَتَصْرَعَكَ

قَالَ فَغَضِبَ زَيْدٌ عِنْدَ ذَلِكَ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَيْسَ الْإِمَامُ مِنَّا مَنْ جَلَسَ فِي بَيْتِهِ وَ أَرْخَى سِتْرَهُ وَ ثَبَّطَ عَنِ الْجِهَادِ وَ لَكِنَّ الْإِمَامَ مِنَّا مَنْ مَنَعَ حَوْزَتَهُ وَ جَاهَدَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ حَقَّ جِهَادِهِ وَ دَفَعَ عَنْ رَعِيَّتِهِ وَ ذَبَّ عَنْ حَرِيمِهِ

قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) هَلْ تَعْرِفُ يَا أَخِي مِنْ نَفْسِكَ شَيْئاً مِمَّا نَسَبْتَهَا إِلَيْهِ فَتَجِي‏ءَ عَلَيْهِ بِشَاهِدٍ مِنْ كِتَابِ اللَّهِ أَوْ حُجَّةٍ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَوْ تَضْرِبَ بِهِ مَثَلًا فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَحَلَّ حَلَالًا وَ حَرَّمَ حَرَاماً وَ فَرَضَ فَرَائِضَ وَ ضَرَبَ أَمْثَالًا وَ سَنَّ سُنَناً وَ لَمْ يَجْعَلِ الْإِمَامَ الْقَائِمَ بِأَمْرِهِ شُبْهَةً فِيمَا فَرَضَ لَهُ مِنَ الطَّاعَةِ أَنْ يَسْبِقَهُ بِأَمْرٍ قَبْلَ مَحَلِّهِ أَوْ يُجَاهِدَ فِيهِ قَبْلَ حُلُولِهِ

وَ قَدْ قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي الصَّيْدِ لا تَقْتُلُوا الصَّيْدَ وَ أَنْتُمْ حُرُمٌ أَ فَقَتْلُ الصَّيْدِ أَعْظَمُ أَمْ قَتْلُ النَّفْسِ الَّتِي حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ وَ جَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ مَحَلًّا وَ قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ إِذا حَلَلْتُمْ فَاصْطادُوا وَ قَالَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لا تُحِلُّوا شَعائِرَ اللَّهِ وَ لَا الشَّهْرَ الْحَرامَ فَجَعَلَ الشُّهُورَ عِدَّةً مَعْلُومَةً فَجَعَلَ مِنْهَا أَرْبَعَةً حُرُماً وَ قَالَ فَسِيحُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ أَرْبَعَةَ أَشْهُرٍ وَ اعْلَمُوا أَنَّكُمْ غَيْرُ مُعْجِزِي اللَّهِ

ثُمَّ قَالَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى فَإِذَا انْسَلَخَ الْأَشْهُرُ الْحُرُمُ فَاقْتُلُوا الْمُشْرِكِينَ حَيْثُ وَجَدْتُمُوهُمْ فَجَعَلَ لِذَلِكَ مَحَلًّا وَ قَالَ وَ لا تَعْزِمُوا عُقْدَةَ النِّكاحِ حَتَّى يَبْلُغَ الْكِتابُ أَجَلَهُ فَجَعَلَ لِكُلِّ شَيْ‏ءٍ أَجَلًا وَ لِكُلِّ أَجَلٍ كِتَاباً

فَإِنْ كُنْتَ عَلَى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّكَ وَ يَقِينٍ مِنْ أَمْرِكَ وَ تِبْيَانٍ مِنْ شَأْنِكَ فَشَأْنَكَ وَ إِلَّا فَلَا تَرُومَنَّ أَمْراً أَنْتَ مِنْهُ فِي شَكٍّ وَ شُبْهَةٍ وَ لَا تَتَعَاطَ زَوَالَ مُلْكٍ لَمْ تَنْقَضِ أُكُلُهُ وَ لَمْ يَنْقَطِعْ مَدَاهُ وَ لَمْ يَبْلُغِ الْكِتَابُ أَجَلَهُ فَلَوْ قَدْ بَلَغَ مَدَاهُ وَ انْقَطَعَ أُكُلُهُ وَ بَلَغَ الْكِتَابُ أَجَلَهُ لَانْقَطَعَ الْفَصْلُ وَ تَتَابَعَ النِّظَامُ وَ لَأَعْقَبَ اللَّهُ فِي التَّابِعِ وَ الْمَتْبُوعِ الذُّلَّ وَ الصَّغَارَ

أَعُوذُ بِاللَّهِ مِنْ إِمَامٍ ضَلَّ عَنْ وَقْتِهِ فَكَانَ التَّابِعُ فِيهِ أَعْلَمَ مِنَ الْمَتْبُوعِ أَ تُرِيدُ يَا أَخِي أَنْ تُحْيِيَ مِلَّةَ قَوْمٍ قَدْ كَفَرُوا بِآيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَ عَصَوْا رَسُولَهُ وَ اتَّبَعُوا أَهْوَاءَهُمْ بِغَيْرِ هُدًى مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ ادَّعَوُا الْخِلَافَةَ بِلَا بُرْهَانٍ مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ لَا عَهْدٍ مِنْ رَسُولِهِ

أُعِيذُكَ بِاللَّهِ يَا أَخِي أَنْ تَكُونَ غَداً الْمَصْلُوبَ بِالْكُنَاسَةِ ثُمَّ ارْفَضَّتْ عَيْنَاهُ وَ سَالَتْ دُمُوعُهُ ثُمَّ قَالَ اللَّهُ بَيْنَنَا وَ بَيْنَ مَنْ هَتَكَ سِتْرَنَا وَ جَحَدَنَا حَقَّنَا وَ أَفْشَى سِرَّنَا وَ نَسَبَنَا إِلَى غَيْرِ جَدِّنَا وَ قَالَ فِينَا مَا لَمْ نَقُلْهُ فِي أَنْفُسِنَا .
